name: Lu's Chicken Cordon Bleu
recipeIngredient: 2 whole chicken breasts, boned and skinned
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each chicken breast in half.
2. Pound each breast half flat between two sheets of waxed paper.
3. Spread mustard down the center of each breast and layer with 1 ham slice and 1 cheese slice.
4. Fold the breast half around the filling, starting with one long side, then short sides, finishing with a long side to ensure the filling is completely covered.
5. Press edges together and roll each piece in waxed paper.
6. Season flour with salt and pepper.
7. Beat egg lightly with 1 teaspoon water.
8. Line up plates: a plate of seasoned flour, a shallow dish with beaten egg, and another plate with bread crumbs.
9. Roll each chicken piece first in flour to coat, then dip in the egg mixture, coating completely, and finally roll in bread crumbs, pressing crumbs into the egg coating.
10. Place chicken pieces on a tray and refrigerate for one hour.
11. Heat peanut oil in a large frying pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the pan.
12. Fry chicken pieces in batches, two at a time, until browned evenly on all sides; about 2 minutes per side.
13. Remove from oil, drain on paper towels, and place in a non-stick baking dish.
14. Bake at 350°F for 15 minutes.
